title: Why I'm Back in THAILAND + Another Taxi Scam??
text: Hey guys, how's it going? Good afternoon. Today is Sunday, April 12th, 2026. Currently 88° Fahrenheit, that is 31° C. It is flipping hot here in Thailand, but right here it is nice and pleasant in this shade and with the sea breeze blowing at me. I'm looking at the sea and the beach straight ahead here in Jomtien, which is very close to Pattaya. About 2 hours from Bangkok. So, I haven't filmed in 5 days or so. And so, basically in this video I'm going to catch you guys up with uh a number of things. Why I decided to come back to Thailand, other than the obvious that it is a great place to come back to again and again, but uh there is more than that to my reasons. And also tell a few stories. There was another stressful taxi situation, etc. While I walk along the street there, right along the beach, which will give you a good idea of what is happening here in Jomtien, an area that I hadn't really known about. I only went to Pattaya once. That was like exactly a decade ago in spring of 2016. And only stayed there for a couple of days, not really my scene. But I learned about Jomtien as a mellower alternative, thanks to the videos of William Taudien who uh has spent a lot of time in Thailand and Mexico. And I saw one of his videos showing the nice resort that he was staying at at a great price with a nice pool and uh easy access to the beach and all the things that you love about Thailand. Markets, for example, this one right here, a night market, so not happening at the moment, but uh seems like they're kind of just starting to get ready. I ate here last night. I will stop and show you what I got. And then the uh beach right out there. I'm just going to uh walk through the market for now and then get uh back to the explanations, etc. So, when I was here yesterday around 7:30 or so, it was surprisingly quiet. Because these kinds of markets are usually a really busy, bustling scene, but I guess it's going into low season here. Maybe for a few different reasons, because it's heating up here, because the uh winter is over in Europe and North America, and so less uh tourists coming, perhaps. Sawatdee krap. So, that's what I had last night, the noodles with mixed sauce and then some uh dumplings. Not those kinds of bun, but uh not the wontons either, different one. Anyways, typical like potstickers. So, yeah, good meal there for 240 baht for the two dishes, which is about $7.50. So, I just spent a month in India. Had another great trip. Went to some really interesting places. And of course, there is always much more to see in India. I could have just stayed there for a while longer. I had no restrictions as far as my visa or the prices or anything. Of course, it's nice and cheap there. But for one thing, it was getting hot in the south. So, that was one factor was basically everywhere except the Himalayas in India is getting hotter and hotter day by day. Not that it's any cooler here, but uh basically, if I was going to stay in India for much longer, then it would have been a matter of heading up into the Himalayas. But there was a factor that ended up being an important one, which is what's happening with the war in Iran. And so, it is a really, really unknown situation as far as the global impacts that are going to be affecting various places and are affecting many places now because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. I'm sure that many of you are fully aware of this. It has been all over the news. Hormuz in the news because 20% of the world's oil supply has to pass through the Strait of Hormuz on oil tankers. And it has been mostly closed for almost 6 weeks now. And that is why the price of gas at your local gas station has gone up. In a nutshell, it's because of the Strait of Hormuz. And so, the big wild card is how much longer is it going to be closed for, because it doesn't just affect the price of gasoline at the pump, it affects the price of pretty much everything because you have to haul stuff in a vehicle and pay for gas in order to get it to where you can buy it. And it jacks up the prices of various goods, whether they are directly made from an oil product, plastics, etc., or because of shipping costs and whatnot. And so, basically, in a nutshell, then this is having a serious impact on the global economy and it could have a much worse impact going forward. So, this is where I had breakfast with William Taudien a few hours ago. Here at uh Daisy D's. They make a really good English breakfast. And they have lots of fans cooling things down. I went there yesterday and then said to William, "Want to meet me for uh breakfast there?" Things are so unknown and unpredictable right now with the global economy. And of course, one thing that can be seriously impacted is the price of flights. And I've been seeing various videos projecting what could possibly happen, talking about supply chain shortages in many Asian countries and uh how the full effects of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz hasn't hit yet, because the oil tankers move so slowly that there are oil tankers carrying oil still in the oceans from 6 weeks ago before the Hormuz Strait closed that are on their way to deliver the oil. And so, the global shipment of oil actually hasn't really impacted the world yet, because the shipments have still been out there crossing the oceans. But of course, there aren't the new tankers full of oil continuing after that point. And so, the full effects could hit at some point in the near future in the course of the month of April and going into May. And so, taking all this into account, basically I decided that I wanted to be somewhere a little more stable and mellow than either a crazy Indian city or an out of the way place in India. Just in case there are issues with fuel fuel shortages flights getting cancelled or prices skyrocketing etc. I decided it would be a good idea to be somewhere a little bit uh stabler and close to an international airport where I have better access to uh more flights for a little while at least whatever 2 3 weeks and just kind of see what's going to happen get a better gauge of where things are going. And also just to kind of consider where I want to go next from here going into the spring and sort of ponder my options etc. So instead of continuing my travels around India then decided to come here just kind of paying for a bit and uh watch the news take a break probably won't film as much and uh try to figure out my plans going forwards. And so I mentioned uh that I learned about Jomtien here from William Taudien but I wasn't actually sure if he was going to be here when I arrived. I had exchanged a couple of messages with him weeks ago. Then I continued my travels around India and then he posted a video from the Philippines and so I thought maybe he had already left and went to the Philippines. And so I decided to just get here and then see if he was around. And so I got here sent him a message and then it turned out that uh he was back from the Philippines cuz his videos are delayed and so he is in fact here. And so we hung out a couple times over the past few days. But I need to uh update with the uh getting here and the taxi ride. So here you can see a spectacular big beach. So that is definitely one of the uh draws of uh this area is a big long I mean kilometers long beach. But it's a little mellower scene than Pattaya which is that way like a 15 20 minute drive. And then also a factor is just that the prices are so incredibly reasonable. So I showed my room there $35 for a night. That is a big huge resort with a huge pool and a gym. I stayed at a different place my first few nights which was even cheaper and as it turned out it was actually nicer. And so I'll show a video that I took of my room at the other place. And that was only 26 bucks US per night. And they actually have more facilities. They have a hot tub sauna steam room which they don't have at the place that I'm staying now. And so those prices are just amazing for uh being in a more developed country here in in Thailand as opposed to in India for example where I was paying about the same amount I think in Goa. Actually I think I paid even more at a place where they had a tiny little pool. Here they have a massive pool that I can swim my laps in. And so that was a draw was to get back to somewhere where the pool where I could get back to swimming every day cuz I really enjoy that. And so it's kind of the whole mix of reasons to hang here for a little while. Also I'd never been here. I've been to Pattaya but not to Jomtien. As you can see it's you know a classic touristy beach scene with your usual stuff 7-Eleven and street food and lots of bars big hotels. And so I'm really not sure how long I'm going to stay here. There you can see a bunch of pools. So there's lots of stuff to do around here beyond just the town and the beach. I guess there must be a zoo. Jomtien Beach Chang Island Koh Si Chang. Now that is different from Koh Chang. I've heard about Koh Si Chang but not exactly sure where it is. I think that it is very close to here. There's another island Koh Larn or Koh Lan which I went to I think last year. Khao Phra Tamnak. So I've never heard of that before. Mini Siam Pattaya. So there's a lot of stuff going on here much of it very touristy geared towards families. Sanctuary of Truth. So William had recommended going to that and I was actually thinking of going to it today and then saw on the map that it is on the other side of Pattaya City like a 35 minute drive away and I wasn't feeling up for that long of a drive cuz it's like 4:00 in the afternoon now. And so I might go to this other cool looking restaurant that's a little out of the city and go get a drink there just to see something else. Jomtien night market. So I showed a little market at the beginning that's a smaller market but this is a bigger market the main night market. So uh that should be a lot more happening later this evening. Anyways so now I want to uh tell the story of getting here from India. So I was in Aurangabad in kind of central India and then I booked a flight a series of two flights one to Delhi. We have a big squirrel and pigeons. Oh. Oh, we have a bunch of squirrels. Holy moly. And the squirrels are fighting with the pigeons. Oh my gosh. These guys are aggressive looking. They're going for the coconut meat. Look. So there's like dozens of them in this tree. Wild. Oh, a black one. I almost just walked right past this. They look like they want to jump on me. Look at that a jet black squirrel. I don't think I've ever seen that before. Wild. What a scene. I see that's what they're going for is that is. All right. Let's keep on cruising. I booked this series of flights one from Aurangabad to Delhi then a second flight was included that was for the next day in the afternoon arriving here at 8:30 into BKK Bangkok. So I made a point of flying to BKK rather than Don Mueang because BKK airport is closer to Pattaya and Jomtien. And so that flight was landing at 8:30. All right. This is where things are happening it seems. People having uh a late lunch or early dinner or whatever you call it when it's like 4:30 in the afternoon. And so that was about a 3 and 1/2 hour flight direct from Delhi to Bangkok. So I land at 8:30 get my luggage and then ask around if there's still a bus going but there isn't. So it has to be a taxi. And at this point I've already booked my accommodation. So I booked three nights at the other place that I showed with the much nicer view looking down at the uh pool. And so I go out to where the taxis are and there's a kiosk. And you go to the kiosk, you get a ticket, and then it tells you which taxi you're taking. The spots are numbered and there are taxis sitting there. And so you get this ticket with a number and a picture of the taxi driver and his ID number, like his taxi driver number. And so it's all a fully legit situation. And so you think low stress, low risk, no problem. The problem is I get in the taxi and we take off and it's like 5-10 minutes later or something, I realize, wait, where's the meter? And so I can see a box that looks like it's the meter, but there's no numbers. And so this is, you know, after 9:00, so it's dark. And so I can't quite tell if maybe it has the numbers, but it's actually just dark and not lit up because like the light is broken or something. I'm thinking, well, maybe the meter's working, but I just can't really see it well enough or something. But then as we're going under street lights, I get little glimpses of it and realize, okay, no, it's not on. And we hadn't agreed to a price beforehand because I just thought this was obviously going to be a metered taxi ride. And so I'm now stressed out. So once you're in that situation, you have a few options. Basically, you have like two options. Confront the guy there in the taxi and say, what's going on? Where's the meter? Or don't. But this is like an hour and a half taxi ride. And so I'm not sure if this guy is, you know, legit, a scammer, what is really going on. And I'm at the beginning of the taxi ride. And so I don't want to piss him off, you know, create a uh scene, and then I'm stuck with this guy in the taxi for another hour and a half. Or there's a confrontation and he decides to drop me off, dump me because he doesn't like me questioning him about the prices or whatever. And then I'm dumped off in the absolute middle of nowhere in a really bad situation. And so I decide, well, I'm already in it. He hasn't turned on the meter. And so he's not going to want to turn on the meter when we've just driven some distance that wasn't being clocked on the meter. And so I decide, okay, just don't make waves and I'll deal with the situation when I get there. So there is a uh picture, like an ID card with a number on the dashboard of the taxi. So I check that number and then I check the number on the piece of paper that I have, and they're different. And so now I'm even more stressed out wondering what is really going on here. So I look online for how much is the typical price of a taxi from BKK Airport to Jomtien. And it says 1,200 to 1,600 baht. And so that's like 40 to 50-ish dollars, which sounds about right. And then there are some tolls along the way and I think somewhere, maybe on the ticket or something, it mentioned that I would have to pay tolls additionally. So I'm keeping that number in mind. And then at the same time, I basically get all ready for a potential disagreement with the guy. I have my camera all ready to go so that I'm going to uh start filming if when I ask the price after arriving, if it's an exorbitant price, then I'm going to start filming right away. Film the whole thing. Film the piece of paper with the number. Film the ID on the dashboard with the number there. Get it all on camera and then make it clear to him that I am filming and he's on camera, you know, his face and everything. And so don't mess with me. So that I have some, you know, leverage against him trying to rip me off. And so basically I decide if he's going to try to charge me more than 2,000 baht, then that sounds like it's a rip-off. And so I'm just kind of keeping those numbers in mind. So finally we arrive at where I'm staying and I'm really, really curious to hear what number is going to come out of his mouth as far as the total price. And he says 2,200. So I said that anything over 2,000 seemed like it was a rip-off, but only 200 baht over 2,000 isn't much over that. And so that isn't really a number that's big enough for me to make a scene about it. Even though it sounds high. So that is about $68 US for like an hour and 30 to 40-minute taxi ride. Also, there were some tolls added in there. And also it's a night taxi ride leaving at, you know, close to 10:00. Or maybe 9:30. And so I'm not sure if that was a rip-off or if maybe that was actually the legit price when you factor in the tolls and the night fare. But I thought for sure that Bangkok taxis tend to use the meter. Jomtien, Pattaya Beach. I just can't remember thinking back to the last few times that I was in uh Bangkok whether we agreed on a price in advance or if they used a meter. But the reason I didn't ask the price beforehand was because I thought he was going to be using a meter. So uh 2,200, I just pay that. An expensive taxi ride, but not a crazy rip-off. So I don't know if that was maybe a little bit of a scam in which he saw a foreigner and decided not to flip on the meter and then planned to charge me a bit more, but not a lot more. And so he charged an extra 500 baht or something like that, which would be like 15 bucks. Or maybe that was just the legit late night price with tolls. So feel free to uh let me know what you think on that. All right. So there's this uh fancy-looking restaurant that's a bit further away. What does that say? Dongtan? Okay. Haven't heard of that. I guess this is the Dongtan section of Pattaya Beach. So uh maybe what I should have done would used Bolt. I tried Uber, but Uber doesn't work, I guess, in Thailand at all. But uh I got Bolt set up and used it here. And it's really cheap. So I don't know if it will work going from Bangkok Airport all the way out here, but perhaps that could have been a solution. And maybe I'll try that whenever I'm going back to the airport. But anyways, that is how I'm going to now get up to this restaurant is use Bolt. So I think you've definitely gotten a good idea in the course of this walk what is happening here in uh Jomtien. It is a long, long, long stretch of beach with lots of restaurants and shops and resorts along the way. But this uh restaurant that I'm trying to get to looks uh quite unique. So let's get up there. And car ordered pick up in 5 minutes. It's going to cost 123 baht, which is about $4 for I think a 10-minute ride. And while I'm waiting, let's get a closer shot of the water here. A little windy. Kind of choppy. Not the uh clearest-looking water, but uh still pretty nice. So, I think that is Koh Lan island, which I went to about a year ago. Pretty cool island, especially for being so close to Pattaya and Jomtien here. A short ferry ride over there. So, this is the restaurant. The Forest by the Sky. So, this place is pretty swanky-looking. Drinks are 300 baht. 10 bucks. Not surprised. So, there's that really nice-looking beach there. And the sun a-settin'.
